title: CI Matrices, Required Checks, Caching, and Merge Policy · Policy Clinic: Cache Artifacts
summary: Build a secure required-check workflow across supported environments. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates cache artifacts increment in a required CI quality-gate workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use dependency caches and test artifacts without trusting executable output across boundaries.
Test declared runtime, operating-system, database, and feature support intentionally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Cache Artifacts evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-cache-artifacts-8umsgm: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Cache Artifacts into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-cache-artifacts-8umsgm:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
